choices.text: `[slug]` matches exactly one segment (params.slug is a string). `[...slug]` is catch-all — matches one or more segments (params.slug is an array). `[[...slug]]` is optional catch-all — matches zero or more segments, including the bare path. Example: with `[slug]`, /docs and /docs/a/b don't match. With `[...slug]`, /docs/a/b matches but /docs alone doesn't. With `[[...slug]]`, /docs (slug undefined) AND /docs/a/b (slug = ['a','b']) both match. Pick based on URL depth flexibility needed.

questions.prompt: Pre-rendering 5,000 blog posts but want to add new ones without rebuilding the entire site. Setup?

choices.text: Export `generateStaticParams()` returning the slugs of all existing posts; combine with `export const revalidate = 60` (or another time) for ISR. At build time, 5,000 posts are pre-rendered. When a request comes for a new slug not in the build set, `dynamicParams = true` (the default) renders it on-demand and caches it. Existing posts are served from CDN; new ones generate at first hit then cache. Best of static + dynamic without rebuilding the whole site.

questions.explanation: generateStaticParams for existing + dynamicParams=true + revalidate for new posts on-demand.
